reasoning:
The baseline price (the last available data before/on 2026-03-01) is $71.32 from Friday, Feb 27. Over the weekend of March 1, major news broke of a US-Israeli conflict with Iran disrupting Middle East shipments, driving over-the-counter Brent crude prices toward $80/bbl. Given the magnitude of this geopolitical shock involving a major OPEC producer, it is overwhelmingly likely that spot prices will jump upon market open on Monday and remain well above the $71.32 baseline. 

For the short horizon of March 8 (effectively trading data from Friday, March 6), the shock will still be fresh and risk premiums will remain very high, making a prompt mean-reversion below $71.32 extremely unlikely. I estimate this at 0.92. 

For the March 31 horizon, there is more time for potential de-escalation, rapid diplomatic resolution, or further OPEC+ supply interventions (they announced a 206k bpd output increase starting in April). While prices usually retain a geopolitical risk premium for months after such a shock, the chance of a partial mean reversion or resolution increases with time. I estimate this at 0.82.
